source: TrichoScan AI PRD v1.1 - Unified 54-Q Matrix

questions.desc: Age directly affects follicle sensitivity and recovery capacity
Determines the hormonal hair loss pathway and staging system used
Humidity, pollution, and UV levels all affect scalp oil balance
Stress exposure and physical environment factor into hair health
Trapped heat and scalp friction accelerate follicle miniaturisation
The single strongest predictor of androgenetic alopecia
Prior treatment response guides future protocol selection
Hypo/hyperthyroidism is a leading cause of diffuse hair loss
Post-illness telogen effluvium causes sudden mass shedding 6–12 weeks later
BP meds, antidepressants, isotretinoin can all cause hair loss
PCOS-related androgens directly cause female pattern thinning
The pattern determines Norwood/Ludwig staging and treatment protocol
Self-assessed severity guides urgency of clinical intervention
Sudden onset suggests telogen effluvium; gradual onset suggests AGA

questions.text: How Long Has Hair Loss Been Occurring?
questions.desc: Duration directly correlates with follicle survivability
Multi-site involvement suggests Alopecia Areata or hormonal cause
Perceived density compared to your hair 5–10 years ago
Visible scalp index correlates strongly with follicle density loss

options.label: Slightly visible only in bright light

questions.desc: Most affected zone in male pattern baldness
Mid-scalp loss indicates advancing AGA progression
Crown is typically the last zone to respond to medical therapy

sections.title: Hair Shaft Quality
sections.subtitle: Evaluating hair fiber health and integrity

questions.desc: Miniaturised fine hairs signal advanced follicle weakening from DHT

questions.text: Do You Notice Finer, Shorter Hairs Near Hairline?
questions.desc: Miniaturisation is the hallmark clinical sign of androgenetic alopecia

options.label: Slight — few thinner hairs at hairline

questions.desc: Mid-shaft breakage is distinct from root loss and indicates shaft damage
Indicates cuticle damage and overall shaft integrity
Texture changes signal nutritional deficiency or chemical/heat damage
Sebum imbalance disrupts the follicle microenvironment
Malassezia fungus overgrowth causes dandruff and scalp inflammation
Yellow oily scales indicate seborrheic dermatitis — a treatable condition

options.label: No — only white flakes if any

questions.desc: Persistent itching signals active inflammation directly harming follicles
Hard water deposits block follicles and cause mineral buildup

questions.text: Smooth, Shiny Scalp Patches with Zero Hair Regrowth?
questions.desc: Scarring alopecia permanently destroys follicles — early detection is critical to halt progression

options.label: None — all areas have some regrowth potential
Possibly — 1–2 small smooth patches with no regrowth
Yes — clearly visible smooth / tight scalp areas
Yes — confirmed scarring alopecia by dermatologist / biopsy

sections.title: Hair Fall Severity
sections.subtitle: Quantifying active shedding and loss rate

questions.desc: Normal is 50–100 strands/day; above this is pathological
Increased shedding during wash is an early diagnostic sign
Nocturnal shedding indicates an active effluvium phase

questions.text: Gentle Pull Test — Grasp 40–50 hairs, pull firmly
questions.desc: More than 3 strands indicates active telogen effluvium
Chronic loss typically indicates AGA; acute suggests a trigger event
Cortisol elevation is proven to push follicles into the telogen (shedding) phase
Growth hormone and melatonin released during sleep are critical for follicle repair
Nutrient-poor diets deprive follicles of essential building blocks

options.label: Average — mix of healthy and junk

questions.desc: Hair is 95% keratin — protein deficiency directly weakens follicle output
Dehydration affects scalp microcirculation and toxin clearance
Nicotine constricts scalp blood vessels — a proven hair loss accelerant
Anabolic steroids and testosterone boosters significantly raise DHT
Regular exercise improves scalp circulation and reduces cortisol
Overwashing strips natural oils; underwashing causes product buildup
Regular light oiling improves circulation; heavy overnight oiling can clog follicles
Excessive UV causes oxidative stress on scalp and follicles
Helps personalise the treatment plan to your specific objective
Postpartum hair loss typically begins 2–4 months after delivery and lasts 6–12 months
Irregular or absent periods often signal hormonal imbalances

questions.text: Oral Contraceptive Pill (OCP) / Hormonal Contraception Use
questions.desc: Certain progestin-dominant OCPs have androgenic activity

questions.text: Significant Weight Loss in Past 12 Months
questions.desc: Rapid weight loss is a documented trigger for telogen effluvium

options.label: Crash / very low calorie diet (< 1,000 kcal/day)

questions.desc: PCOS causes elevated androgens which trigger female androgenetic alopecia
